summary: Add the Opportunity Detail UI slice with persisted score/risk/monetization/SERP context and limited Save, Discard, and Build Next status updates.
acceptance_criteria: A server-rendered /opportunities/[id] page exists and loads one persisted Opportunity by id through the shared opportunity data helper.

The detail page renders the opportunity title/keyword, score, score breakdown/explanation where data exists, SERP weakness summary, tool concept or toolability summary, monetization cues, risk cues, build complexity, kill criteria, radar task/search run context, current status, and navigation back to the list/dashboard.
Missing opportunities render the framework not-found path or an equivalent 404 behavior without leaking internal errors.

A narrow local status update path exists for saved, discarded, and build_next only; invalid statuses and missing opportunities return safe errors, and successful updates persist the new Opportunity status.
The status update UI is clear and restrained, reflects the current status, and does not imply public auth, multi-user workflow, revenue validation, SEO certainty, or market proof.
Shared view models remain plain serializable objects; UI components do not import Prisma or duplicate authoritative query/update logic.

Focused automated tests cover detail view-model serialization, score breakdown/risk/monetization rendering, missing opportunity behavior where practical, valid and invalid status updates, and no-op scope boundaries.
README documents the Opportunity Detail and status-update slice, validation commands, local single-admin assumption, and explicit non-goals.

No MVP Spec Agent, /api/opportunities/[id]/mvp-spec endpoint, Copy MVP Spec behavior, schema migration, package dependency, provider setup, scan orchestration, scoring formula change, auth/session system, deployment, or GitHub Actions change is implemented.
notes: Derived from docs/09_codex_implementation_tasks.md Task 13 and docs/11_development_plan.md M5.
Task 14/M6 owns MVP Spec generation and Copy Markdown. This task may show placeholders or absence of MVP Spec content only if it does not implement the generation/copy workflow.
systems_architect is required because this introduces a dynamic detail route and a local mutation boundary over persisted Opportunity status.
domain_designer is required because status wording and score/risk presentation must not overstate local/mock scan output.
security_reviewer is required because this task adds or exposes a write path, even though the current MVP remains a local single-admin application.
non_goals: No MVP Spec Agent, generated Markdown, Copy MVP Spec button behavior, or /api/opportunities/[id]/mvp-spec route.
No Prisma schema changes, migrations, seed changes, package changes, real provider setup, LLM setup, env secret loading, auth/session, deployment, or GitHub Actions.
No Run Scan button wiring, RecentRunStatus component, dashboard scan trigger, cron, background jobs, Radar Task CRUD changes, scan orchestration changes, scoring formula changes, or agent changes.
No multi-user audit trail, discard reason workflow, recommendation-learning loop, public deployment policy, or irreversible/delete mutation.
